schema.title: metric / dashboard topic
schema.config_params: list of related IoTDB config parameters
schema.anomaly_classes: subset of {compaction, export, flush, full_cpu, full_memory, network_bandwidth2}
schema.iotdb_subsystems: subset of {Flush, Compaction, MemTable, TsFile, WAL, Memory, Network, Compression, Query, Cache, Procedure, Schema, GC, Election}

tuples.title: Enable Prometheus metric exposition on DataNode
tuples.symptom: No metrics available for anomaly diagnosis; cannot retrieve runtime numbers
tuples.root_cause: DataNode's metric reporter is disabled or set below CORE level; without scraping endpoint Grafana cannot visualize
fix: Set dn_metric_reporter_list=PROMETHEUS in iotdb-system.properties
Set dn_metric_level=CORE (minimum) or IMPORTANT/NORMAL/ALL for richer signal
Set dn_metric_prometheus_reporter_port=9091 (default)
Verify with curl http://<host>:9091/metrics

tuples.title: Storage Engine dashboard: file_count and TsFile size at various stages
tuples.symptom: file_count{name='seq'} or file_count{name='unseq'} climbing; many small TsFiles accumulating; compaction tasks pile up
tuples.root_cause: TsFiles produced by flush faster than the cross-space / inner-space compaction can merge them; compaction throughput is the bottleneck
fix: Increase compaction concurrency (target_compaction_file_size, concurrent_compaction_thread)
Verify disk IO is not saturated (full_cpu / disk i/o wait)
If files accumulate during high write burst, check whether flush is also lagging

tuples.title: WAL dashboard: WAL file count/size and SyncBuffer latency
tuples.symptom: Large number of WAL files; high WAL flush SyncBuffer latency; rising disk usage in wal/ directory
tuples.root_cause: WAL not rotated/flushed in time; disk fsync slow; or wal_throttle_threshold reached because MemTables not flushing fast enough
fix: Check disk IO latency on WAL volume
Inspect MemTable flush queue length to see if flush is back-pressured
Tune wal_throttle_threshold and wal_buffer_size
Force FLUSH ON CLUSTER to drain MemTables and let WAL rotate

tuples.title: Data Writing dashboard: write latency P50/P99 spikes
tuples.symptom: Average / P99 write latency increases; clients see Timeout exceptions or 408/503-like errors
tuples.root_cause: Either MemTable insertion blocked by GC, by WAL throttle, by flush back-pressure, or by Ratis/IoTConsensus log replication delay
fix: Cross-check JVM GC time percentage on system dashboard (full_memory)
Cross-check WAL SyncBuffer latency (flush)
Cross-check network packet rate (network_bandwidth2)
Profile a slow write with DEBUG SQL or query trace

tuples.title: System Monitoring dashboard: JVM GC time % and heap memory usage
tuples.symptom: GC time percentage > 10%; Old-Gen approaches max heap; Full GC frequency rises
tuples.root_cause: Heap too small for workload; MemTable + query temp + cache evicts too slowly; possible memory leak in plugin/UDF
fix: Increase IOTDB_HEAP_SIZE (datanode-env.sh)
Reduce write_memory_proportion if read-heavy, or chunk_timeseriesmeta_free_memory_proportion if memory-pressured queries
Check user-defined UDFs / triggers for leaks via jcmd / heap dump

tuples.title: System Monitoring dashboard: network transmission/packet rate
tuples.symptom: Network bandwidth saturated; packet send/recv rate plateau matches NIC capacity; high TCP retransmit
tuples.root_cause: Insufficient bandwidth between DataNodes for IoTConsensus log replication, or client write fanout exceeding NIC; can also be caused by Ratis election storm
fix: Inspect IoTConsensus / Ratis log dispatcher metrics for backlogs
Throttle clients or shard the workload across more DataNodes
If election storm, raise heartbeat_interval_in_ms and verify NTP clock skew

tuples.title: System Monitoring dashboard: disk space / file count alert
tuples.symptom: Disk usage near threshold (default 5%); system auto-switches to READ-ONLY; writes rejected
tuples.root_cause: DiskSpaceWarningThreshold reached because compaction not freeing space or because WAL/TsFile accumulation outpaces deletion
fix: Free disk space (truncate /logs, archive cold data, drop obsolete databases)
Trigger START REPAIR DATA to clean orphaned TsFiles
Verify compaction is actually producing fewer/larger files
Use SET SYSTEM TO RUNNING after disk recovers

tuples.title: Cluster Overview dashboard: per-node CPU usage
tuples.symptom: One DataNode shows sustained >80% CPU; peers idle; write/query slows down for clients pinned to that node
tuples.root_cause: Data hot-spot (one DataRegion overloaded), or compaction thread saturation, or query without index causing full scans
fix: Use SHOW REGIONS to identify hot DataRegion(s) and rebalance via region migration
Cap concurrent_compaction_thread on hot node
Inspect SHOW QUERIES for long-running queries; KILL QUERY if needed
